title: Test Module: How are farmers in India’s wheat belt adapting to a changing climate?

cover.alt: A barren field with a hand extended, gesturing towards the viewer.

discovery.abstract: This is a test module. Portions of the text are borrowed from a Michigan Sustainability case available at https://www.learngala.com/cases/growing-into-an-uncertain-future.  An introduction to climate change and agriculture in India's wheat belt, covering historical shifts in the Indo-Gangetic Plains, impacts on farmers, and adaptation choices.

audience.description: Accessible to readers interested in climate and agriculture

learning_objectives.text: Describe how climate change is affecting wheat production and farmers in India.
Explain historical changes in land use and agriculture in the Indo-Gangetic Plains.
Identify adaptation strategies and trade-offs that farmers may consider in response to climate stress.
